postcard-002.webp.suggested_fix: We desperately need SNAP! 47 million people — maybe more — are facing starvation and poverty in this great nation of ours — one that supposedly cares about the welfare of all its citizenry. Angela B. Pittsburgh, PA

postcard-143.webp.suggested_fix: To Brooke Rollins, The thought of having SNAP benefits cut is not only incredibly sad but its unethical. Families like mine, a single parent household, rely on these benefits to feed our children. You have an opportunity to do the right thing and continue your duty of servicing the publics need. I hope you make the right choice. - Cierra, former SNAP recipient

postcard-146.webp.suggested_fix: Secretary Brooke Rollins, With great power comes great responsibility. Please do not cut programs that feed millions of Americans every day. Please use your power to protect our nation's most vulnerable. — Jennifer D. Charleston, SC

SNAP is RAIN in food Deserts. Without SNAP, many rural grocery stores would not be able to stay open, leaving whole communities in food deserts. MB - Chas. SC

To: Secretary Brooke Rollins USDA

postcard-148.webp.suggested_fix: The average SNAP recipient receives $6 per day. & most are employed. SNAP is necessary when companies are allowed to pay workers less than a living wage. Punish the companies taking advantage - not the hard working people. Melanie J - Charleston, SC

issues: Last name appears to be 'Summerville' not 'Summenille'
Minor spelling: 'devasting' should likely be 'devastating' (though this matches the handwriting)
postcard-150.webp.suggested_fix: Dear Secretary Rollins, I am writing because I grew up hungry. My single mother and disabled grandmother worked hard to care for 4 children. SNAP was the only thing between us and extreme poverty. Please don't take away this vital and necessary program that helps so many hardworking families and innocent children. I work for a food bank now to give back and see the devastating effects of hunger. The data is clear that SNAP is the most effective anti-poverty tool. Please do the right thing. Pleading, Misty B. Summerville, South Carolina

issues: The word 'negociable' appears to be misspelled - should be 'negotiable'
postcard-152.webp.suggested_fix: No one deserves to go hungry! To have food is not negotiable! Maria B. Madison, WI

postcard-154.webp.suggested_fix: Most of the people facing hunger in the U.S. are children, seniors, or people with disabilities. They are often our most vulnerable neighbors — those least able to absorb cuts to federal food assistance programs. It's not just numbers. It's people. — McKenna M.
